title: Came West to better his condition; took Union Pacific from Omaha, Nebraska
description: Came West to better his condition, and did a little. Took Union Pacific from Omaha, Nebraska. People who took the train were mainly "ruffians" with "no solid holdings." He couldn't sleep on the train. They sold everything but their clothes and bedding. New scenery kept him occupied on the train.

title: Coming to Cedar Ridge from Dayton; hard traveling, hard winter
description: Coming to Cedar Ridge from Dayton. Had to cut brush to be able to turn wagon around. It was harder on their family because they had to take what was left. Discouraged the first winter. Working out on farms. Clearing land. Neighbor's slash fire.

title: Neighbors helped back and forth; wished he rented land instead of homesteading
description: Neighbors helped back and forth. He wished he had rented land instead of homesteading by clearing land. Removes files from market. The extremely long day's drive to and from town.

title: Little work for people to do; took two neighbors to land office in Lewiston to prove you were homesteading; Wells' homestead
description: Little work for people to do for pay. You took two neighbors to show you'd proved up on homestead to the land office in Lewiston ( a "hot town"). He gave up homestead to live with mother after father died. The Wells' homesteaded; that was the way to get a home.

title: Lawsuit after he relinquished the homestead; received no money for the years of work; reservation opening helped local economy
description: Lawsuit after he relinquished the homestead; he got no money for the years of work. People were, "crowding the edges of the reservation before it was opened. Indians were peaceable about it. People generally respected the Indians. Reservation opening helped local economy; people who lost their mortgaged farms got a new start.

title: People could get by with a hundred dollars a year if they gardened. Threshing gave him the money.
description: People could get by with a hundred dollars a year if they gardened. Threshing gave him the money.

title: Eight pounds of coffee for a dollar, wheat for stock only.
description: Eight pounds of coffee for a dollar, wheat for stock only.

title: Party games; schooling; people are unhappy; father dies
description: Party games; chance to cheat and get the girl. Kids were brought up "with a saw and axe". Schooling. Getting by. People aren't happy now. Struggle after father died. One room cabin.
